task_completion.description: Verify that the target directory is configured for FIM realtime monitoring.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a configuration file (like ossec.conf) showing a <directories> tag that includes 'realtime="yes"' (and ideally 'check_all="yes"' or 'check_perm="yes"') for the path '/opt/secure_configs'.

task_completion.description: Verify the custom rule has the correct ID, level, and parent rule.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for local_rules.xml showing a <rule> tag with id="110001" and level="12". Inside this rule block, there must be an <if_sid>550</if_sid> tag.

task_completion.description: Verify the custom rule targets the correct directory and permission regex.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the rule 110001 block in local_rules.xml, look for conditions matching the directory '/opt/secure_configs' (e.g., <match> or <field name="syscheck.path">) and the exact regex 'w.$' for permissions (e.g., <field name="syscheck.perm_after">w.$</field> or <regex>w.$</regex>).

task_completion.description: Verify the custom rule has the exact required description.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the rule 110001 block in local_rules.xml, look for the tag: <description>Critical: File became world-writable in secure directory</description>.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent triggered the alert by changing a file's permissions to 777 in the target directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal command executing 'chmod 777' on a file located within '/opt/secure_configs'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the alert was successfully generated and captured.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for terminal output (e.g., tailing /var/ossec/logs/alerts/alerts.log or alerts.json) or a Wazuh dashboard screenshot showing an alert triggered for rule ID 110001.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent edited the actual Wazuh configuration files, not dummy files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check terminal prompts, file paths in text editors (like nano/vim headers), or cat commands to ensure files edited are in the actual Wazuh directory (e.g., /var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf and /var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml).

integrity.description: Ensure the Wazuh manager/agent was restarted to apply the new configurations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for terminal commands like 'systemctl restart wazuh-manager', 'systemctl restart wazuh-agent', or '/var/ossec/bin/wazuh-control restart'.

integrity.description: Ensure the generated alert is authentic and not manually fabricated.
integrity.visual_evidence: The alert output should display standard Wazuh log formatting (JSON structure or standard alert format with timestamps, agent info, and syscheck metadata), rather than a simple echo'd string.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Directory: '/opt/secure_configs'. Rule ID: 110001. Parent Rule ID: 550 (Wazuh's standard 'Integrity checksum changed' event). Alert Level: 12. Permission Regex: 'w.$' (matches world-writable permissions in Wazuh's 9-character format). Exact Description: 'Critical: File became world-writable in secure directory'.
